elements.text: Dust motes drift through the high, narrow windows of the Ironheart Citadel vault. On the massive stone table, ancient, crumbling scrolls of forbidden sects lie pinned beneath charcoal-rubbed ciphers. Beside them rests a scrap of oiled parchment—Renna's scavenged reports from the Ashfall borders.
Kaelen traces a scarred finger along a series of recurring geometric glyphs, his eyes shifting rapidly as his perfect memory aligns the texts.
The resource consumption wasn't logistical. The sect wasn't building an empire. They were digging a canal.
They were monsters who slaughtered for territory, Kaelen. We broke them at the gates. It was a righteous defense.
elements.expression: defiant, though her voice trembles slightly

elements.text: Kaelen doesn't look up. He places Renna's intelligence report directly over the sect's map of blood-sacrifices. The patterns match flawlessly.
They were a cell. A single organ in a biology that spans three continents. Every death we dealt only lubricated the mechanism. The blood was meant to flow downward, to the deeper strata.
elements.expression: unemotional, as if explaining a simple engine

elements.text: Downward? To what? We saved those people. There is honor in the blood we spilled to protect them!
elements.expression: with desperate urgency, searching Valerius's face

elements.text: Master Valerius stands in the deep shadows of the stone pillars, his hands resting heavily on his worn sword-hilt. His face is lined with ancient, exhausting grief.
The girl speaks of honor because she has not yet smelled the rot in the soil, Kaelen. But you have. You see the shape of the mouth that is waiting to open.
It is not a mouth. It is a system of total cultivation. It uses whole generations as compost. The aggressive sect was merely the first layer of soil being turned.
Lyra steps back, her hand slipping from her hilt. She looks at the overlapping maps, the cold math of Kaelen's deductions stripping away the meaning of every sacrifice she witnessed.
No. If this is true... then our victories, our dead... none of it mattered. It was just a trigger?
Valerius steps forward into the dim light, placing a heavy hand on the stone table, his gaze locked on Kaelen.

We have only glimpsed its shadow, boy. The deep roots go further than even your cold mind can calculate. If you seek to dissect this beast, it will require more than logic. It will require every drop of your humanity.
elements.expression: grave, warning, yet fully committing to Kaelen's grim pursuit

elements.text: Kaelen's eyes narrow, his resolve hardening into something utterly devoid of warmth as he stares down at the terrifying scale of the network.
Then I will adapt the budget. We begin preparation immediately.
